title: Security Driver Entry Point
text: Security Driver Entry Point A customized security driver must include the zsecdrv.h and specify a function to serve as an entry point for the driver. Depending on the type of security driver, other definition files should also be included. Explain this code #include "zsecdrv.h" #include "usmtpsecsub.h" #include "unistr.h" #include "unienc.h" extern long EntryPointFunction (USecDrv *SecDrv) Copy Parameters EntryPointFunction For SMTP security drivers, this can be any name. For a client/server security driver, it must be usecappl() . Type Name Direction Description USecDrv * SecDrv INOUT Security Driver Control Structure (USecDrv) Return Values EntryPointFunction Ffunction should return a long value that must be one of the following: Table 1. Return Values for Entry Point Function Return Value (long) Description Action was successful. USEC_INFO Action was successful and an info message is returned. USEC_ERROR Something went wrong and an error message is returned. The user can decide what to do; the action can be retried or not, depending on the application context. If the return value is not USEC_SUCCESS , the driver is also expected to fill the following members of the USecDrv structure: ErrMsg Info message or error message ErrMsgLen Length in bytes of info message or error message Error Info number or error number Description A security driver implementation must be thread-safe. In the Uniface Router, it may be called simultaneously by several threads. Each thread will initialize the driver, and the USecDrv structure will be local to the thread. Related Links Security Driver Control Structure (USecDrv) The security driver control structure is used to exchange information between the driver and the caller. Was this topic helpful?
